data.description: Running `foolery doctor` on Linux throws an ENOENT (file not found) error and exits non-zero before completing its diagnostic output. The command likely references a path that either does not exist on Linux (e.g. a macOS-specific default path) or is not created during installation. The fix should ensure `doctor` handles the missing path gracefully — either by creating it, skipping the check with a clear warning, or correcting the path lookup to use the right platform default.
